title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: Emoji everywhere modern text — social-media posts, product reviews, chat support logs, survey free-text — carry information plain words . Yet summarising emoji corpus surprisingly awkward. Unicode interact cleanly regular expressions, every code point emoji, single visible emoji often built several code points joined together. Counting “many posts contain emoji” “emoji common” hand quickly becomes painful. tidyEmoji removes friction. provides small family verbs take data frame name text column, return tidy data frames drop straight dplyr/ggplot2 workflow: Two design choices worth highlighting: Grapheme-aware detection. Detection performed whole grapheme clusters, skin-tone modifiers (👍🏽) zero-width-joiner sequences family emoji (👨‍👩‍👧‍👦) treated single emoji rather split component parts. illustrated extraction section. Tidy default. Every verb returns tibble, follows verb(data, text_column) convention, supports unquoted column names, functions compose naturally pipe.
code: library(tidyEmoji) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2)

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: Throughout vignette use sample 10,000 tweets collected Atlanta, Georgia. Tweets convenient, emoji-rich example, nothing specific Twitter — data frame text column . actual text lives full_text column, column pass tidyEmoji verb.
code:
ata_tweets <- readr::read_csv("ata_tweets.rda", show_col_types = FALSE) ata_tweets #> # A tibble: 10,000 × 1 #>    full_text                                                                     #>    <chr>                                                                         #>  1 "im not arguing  im not fighting nobody w a gun. people are dying every day … #>  2 "Time left until rump leaves office
Zoom
56days
1349hours
80940minutes
… #>  3 "It’s been 15 years since my father’s imprisonment  I swore it would get eas… #>  4 "Friendsgiving with my roomies🥰"                                             #>  5 "Going to be one of those “countdown app” people just so I have motivation t… #>  6 "When I can turn my philosophical thoughts into poetry. The world gone be on… #>  7 "These females really be trying it but fail to realize my girl tells me ever… #>  8 "why my sister called me toxic lmao"                                          #>  9 "What a great year to be not taking care of no kids."                         #> 10 "definitely don’t see any children in my near future, not with me trying to … #> # ℹ 9,990 more rows

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: emoji_summary() answers first question one usually asks new corpus: much emoji ? returns one-row tibble number entries contain least one emoji total number entries. entry counted regardless many emoji holds. , 2,818 10,000 tweets (28.2%) contain least one emoji.
code: summary_tbl <- ata_tweets %>%   emoji_summary(full_text)  summary_tbl #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   emoji_tweets total_tweets #>          <int>        <int> #> 1         2818        10000

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: emoji_filter() keeps rows whose text contains least one emoji, preserving every original column. useful want compare emoji-bearing emoji-free text, restrict analysis emoji subset. (emoji_tweets() synonym retained backward compatibility.)
code: ata_tweets %>%   emoji_filter(full_text) #> # A tibble: 2,818 × 1 #>    full_text                                                                     #>    <chr>                                                                         #>  1 It’s been 15 years since my father’s imprisonment  I swore it would get easi… #>  2 Friendsgiving with my roomies🥰                                               #>  3 When I can turn my philosophical thoughts into poetry. The world gone be on … #>  4 I always get the last laugh 🥰 so I let people do what they want              #>  5 One thing about me, imma keep that same energy. 🙃                            #>  6 I’m really dope. 😎                                                           #>  7 I miss my bb🥺                                                                #>  8 Got half of bae and raidens Christmas shopping done 😩🙌🏽🙌🏽                    #>  9 Y’all be letting anybody in the studio and ion like dat🥴                     #> 10 Sometimes I need to work on myself before I can please anyone or get ready f… #> # ℹ 2,808 more rows

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: tidyEmoji offers three complementary ways pull emoji text, depending shape output want.

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: emoji_extract_nest() leaves data unchanged except added list-column, .emoji_unicode, holding emoji found row. original data structure preserved, makes convenient intermediate step.
code:
ata_tweets %>%   emoji_extract_nest(full_text) %>%   select(full_text, .emoji_unicode) #> # A tibble: 10,000 × 2 #>    full_text                                                      .emoji_unicode #>    <chr>                                                          <list>         #>  1 "im not arguing  im not fighting nobody w a gun. people are d… <chr [0]>      #>  2 "Time left until rump leaves office
Zoom
56days
1349hours\… <chr [0]>      #>  3 "It’s been 15 years since my father’s imprisonment  I swore i… <chr [1]>      #>  4 "Friendsgiving with my roomies🥰"                              <chr [1]>      #>  5 "Going to be one of those “countdown app” people just so I ha… <chr [0]>      #>  6 "When I can turn my philosophical thoughts into poetry. The w… <chr [1]>      #>  7 "These females really be trying it but fail to realize my gir… <chr [0]>      #>  8 "why my sister called me toxic lmao"                           <chr [0]>      #>  9 "What a great year to be not taking care of no kids."          <chr [0]>      #> 10 "definitely don’t see any children in my near future, not wit… <chr [0]>      #> # ℹ 9,990 more rows

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: emoji_extract_unnest() returns long, tidy table one row per (entry, emoji) pair: row_number records position entry data, .emoji_unicode emoji, .emoji_count many times emoji occurs entry. Entries without emoji dropped. can use plot many emoji emoji-bearing tweet contains:  overwhelming majority emoji tweets carry just one emoji, long, thin tail emoji-heavy tweets.
code: emoji_per_tweet <- ata_tweets %>%   emoji_extract_unnest(full_text)  emoji_per_tweet #> # A tibble: 3,519 × 3 #>    row_number .emoji_unicode .emoji_count #>         <int> <chr>                 <int> #>  1          3 😩                        1 #>  2          4 🥰                        1 #>  3          6 👌🏾                        1 #>  4         11 🥰                        1 #>  5         12 🙃                        1 #>  6         15 😎                        1 #>  7         17 🥺                        1 #>  8         33 😩                        1 #>  9         33 🙌🏽                        2 #> 10         34 🥴                        1 #> # ℹ 3,509 more rows emoji_per_tweet %>%   group_by(row_number) %>%   summarise(n_emoji = sum(.emoji_count)) %>%   ggplot(aes(n_emoji)) +   geom_bar() +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1, 15)) +   labs(x = "Number of emoji in the tweet",        y = "Number of tweets",        title = "Most emoji tweets contain a single emoji")

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: emoji_tokens() produces “one row per emoji occurrence” table — emoji analogue tidy-text token table. keeps original columns adds glyph (.emoji) together name (.emoji_name), category (.emoji_category) sentiment score (.emoji_sentiment). single call gives everything needed counting, joining plotting.
code: ata_tweets %>%   emoji_tokens(full_text) #> # A tibble: 4,611 × 5 #>    full_text                 .emoji .emoji_name .emoji_category .emoji_sentiment #>    <chr>                     <chr>  <chr>       <chr>                      <dbl> #>  1 It’s been 15 years since… 😩     weary face  Smileys & Emot…           -0.368 #>  2 Friendsgiving with my ro… 🥰     smiling fa… Smileys & Emot…           NA     #>  3 When I can turn my philo… 👌🏾     OK hand: m… People & Body             NA     #>  4 I always get the last la… 🥰     smiling fa… Smileys & Emot…           NA     #>  5 One thing about me, imma… 🙃     upside-dow… Smileys & Emot…           NA     #>  6 I’m really dope. 😎       😎     smiling fa… Smileys & Emot…            0.493 #>  7 I miss my bb🥺            🥺     pleading f… Smileys & Emot…           NA     #>  8 Got half of bae and raid… 😩     weary face  Smileys & Emot…           -0.368 #>  9 Got half of bae and raid… 🙌🏽     raising ha… People & Body             NA     #> 10 Got half of bae and raid… 🙌🏽     raising ha… People & Body             NA     #> # ℹ 4,601 more rows

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: Modern emoji frequently composed several code points: base emoji plus skin-tone modifier, several emoji joined zero-width joiners. tidyEmoji detects emoji level grapheme clusters, stay intact. example contains exactly two emoji — one family one thumbs-— tidyEmoji counts rather splitting family four people separating thumb skin tone:
code: demo <- data.frame(   text = c("our family \U0001F468‍\U0001F469‍\U0001F467‍\U0001F466",            "great work \U0001F44D\U0001F3FD") )  demo %>%   emoji_extract_unnest(text) #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   row_number .emoji_unicode .emoji_count #>        <int> <chr>                 <int> #> 1          1 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦                        1 #> 2          2 👍🏽                        1

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: emoji_frequency() counts often emoji appears across whole text column (entry containing emoji twice contributes 2) returns result sorted descending count, annotated emoji’s name, shortcode category.
code: ata_tweets %>%   emoji_frequency(full_text) #> # A tibble: 423 × 5 #>    emoji name                          shortcode                     group     n #>    <chr> <chr>                         <chr>                         <chr> <int> #>  1 😂    face with tears of joy        joy                           Smil…   766 #>  2 😭    loudly crying face            sob                           Smil…   536 #>  3 🤣    rolling on the floor laughing rofl                          Smil…   182 #>  4 😩    weary face                    weary                         Smil…   170 #>  5 🥺    pleading face                 pleading_face                 Smil…   148 #>  6 🥴    woozy face                    woozy_face                    Smil…   136 #>  7 🥰    smiling face with hearts      smiling_face_with_three_hear… Smil…   106 #>  8 🙄    face with rolling eyes        roll_eyes                     Smil…    83 #>  9 💯    hundred points                100                           Smil…    78 #> 10 ❤️     red heart                     heart                         Smil…    66 #> # ℹ 413 more rows

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: need leaders, top_n_emojis() convenience wrapper around emoji_frequency() returns n frequent emoji (default n = 20). Plotting top 20, coloured category, gives immediate sense community expresses :  unicode column holds actual glyph, wish render emoji plot (requires graphics device emoji-capable font). can also request different number emoji:
code: top_20_emojis <- ata_tweets %>%   top_n_emojis(full_text)  top_20_emojis #> # A tibble: 20 × 4 #>    emoji_name                     unicode emoji_category        n #>    <chr>                          <chr>   <chr>             <int> #>  1 joy                            😂      Smileys & Emotion   766 #>  2 sob                            😭      Smileys & Emotion   536 #>  3 rofl                           🤣      Smileys & Emotion   182 #>  4 weary                          😩      Smileys & Emotion   170 #>  5 pleading_face                  🥺      Smileys & Emotion   148 #>  6 woozy_face                     🥴      Smileys & Emotion   136 #>  7 smiling_face_with_three_hearts 🥰      Smileys & Emotion   106 #>  8 roll_eyes                      🙄      Smileys & Emotion    83 #>  9 100                            💯      Smileys & Emotion    78 #> 10 heart                          ❤️       Smileys & Emotion    66 #> 11 thinking                       🤔      Smileys & Emotion    62 #> 12 heart_eyes                     😍      Smileys & Emotion    59 #> 13 unamused                       😒      Smileys & Emotion    52 #> 14 bangbang                       ‼️       Symbols              45 #> 15 relieved                       😌      Smileys & Emotion    44 #> 16 eyes                           👀      People & Body        42 #> 17 pensive                        😔      Smileys & Emotion    41 #> 18 partying_face                  🥳      Smileys & Emotion    40 #> 19 tired_face                     😫      Smileys & Emotion    39 #> 20 sparkles                       ✨      Activities           37 top_20_emojis %>%   mutate(emoji_name = stringr::str_replace_all(emoji_name, "_", " "),          emoji_name = forcats::fct_reorder(emoji_name, n)) %>%   ggplot(aes(n, emoji_name, fill = emoji_category)) +   geom_col() +   labs(x = "Count",        y = NULL,        fill = "Category",        title = "The 20 most frequent emoji") ata_tweets %>%   top_n_emojis(full_text, n = 10) %>%   mutate(emoji_name = stringr::str_replace_all(emoji_name, "_", " "),          emoji_name = forcats::fct_reorder(emoji_name, n)) %>%   ggplot(aes(n, emoji_name, fill = emoji_category)) +   geom_col() +   labs(x = "Count", y = NULL, fill = "Category",        title = "The 10 most frequent emoji")

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: Unicode standard organises emoji 10 categories (see ?category_unicode_crosswalk). emoji_categorize() keeps emoji-bearing rows adds .emoji_category column listing distinct categories present row, separated | row spans one. can tally common category combinations:  count 10 individual categories rather combinations, split .emoji_category strings | tidyr::separate_rows():  “Smileys & Emotion” dominates, followed “People & Body”. Note tweet spanning several categories counted , counts can exceed number emoji tweets.
code: ata_emoji_category <- ata_tweets %>%   emoji_categorize(full_text) %>%   select(.emoji_category)  ata_emoji_category #> # A tibble: 2,818 × 1 #>    .emoji_category                 #>    <chr>                           #>  1 Smileys & Emotion               #>  2 Smileys & Emotion               #>  3 People & Body                   #>  4 Smileys & Emotion               #>  5 Smileys & Emotion               #>  6 Smileys & Emotion               #>  7 Smileys & Emotion               #>  8 Smileys & Emotion|People & Body #>  9 Smileys & Emotion               #> 10 Travel & Places|Activities      #> # ℹ 2,808 more rows ata_emoji_category %>%   count(.emoji_category, sort = TRUE) %>%   filter(n > 20) %>%   mutate(.emoji_category = forcats::fct_reorder(.emoji_category, n)) %>%   ggplot(aes(n, .emoji_category)) +   geom_col() +   labs(x = "Number of tweets", y = NULL,        title = "Most common emoji category combinations") ata_emoji_category %>%   tidyr::separate_rows(.emoji_category, sep = "\\|") %>%   count(.emoji_category, sort = TRUE) %>%   mutate(.emoji_category = forcats::fct_reorder(.emoji_category, n)) %>%   ggplot(aes(n, .emoji_category)) +   geom_col() +   labs(x = "Number of tweets", y = NULL,        title = "Emoji category usage")

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: Emoji strong sentiment signal, emoji_sentiment() surfaces directly. adds .emoji_n (number emoji entry) .emoji_sentiment (mean sentiment emoji, -1 negative +1 positive). Scores come bundled emoji_sentiment_lexicon (described ); entries emoji, whose emoji lexicon, receive NA.
code: ata_sentiment <- ata_tweets %>%   emoji_sentiment(full_text)  ata_sentiment %>%   select(.emoji_n, .emoji_sentiment) #> # A tibble: 10,000 × 2 #>    .emoji_n .emoji_sentiment #>       <int>            <dbl> #>  1        0           NA     #>  2        0           NA     #>  3        1           -0.368 #>  4        1           NA     #>  5        0           NA     #>  6        1           NA     #>  7        0           NA     #>  8        0           NA     #>  9        0           NA     #> 10        0           NA     #> # ℹ 9,990 more rows

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: Looking across tweets contain least one scored emoji:  typical social-media text, emoji sentiment leans strongly positive.
code: ata_sentiment %>%   filter(!is.na(.emoji_sentiment)) %>%   ggplot(aes(.emoji_sentiment)) +   geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.1) +   labs(x = "Mean emoji sentiment",        y = "Number of tweets",        title = "Emoji sentiment skews positive")

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: emoji_tokens() attaches sentiment score every emoji occurrence, can summarise average sentiment category couple lines:
code: ata_tweets %>%   emoji_tokens(full_text) %>%   group_by(.emoji_category) %>%   summarise(mean_sentiment = mean(.emoji_sentiment, na.rm = TRUE),             n_scored = sum(!is.na(.emoji_sentiment))) %>%   filter(n_scored > 0) %>%   mutate(.emoji_category = forcats::fct_reorder(.emoji_category, mean_sentiment)) %>%   ggplot(aes(mean_sentiment, .emoji_category)) +   geom_col() +   labs(x = "Mean sentiment", y = NULL,        title = "Average emoji sentiment by category")

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: scores come emoji_sentiment_lexicon, Emoji Sentiment Ranking Kralj Novak et al. (2015), computed around 70,000 tweets annotated 13 European languages. can work directly — instance, find positive negative reasonably common emoji:
code: emoji_sentiment_lexicon %>%   filter(occurrences >= 500) %>%   slice_max(sentiment_score, n = 8) %>%   select(emoji, unicode_name, occurrences, sentiment_score) #>   emoji         unicode_name occurrences sentiment_score #> 1     ❤    HEAVY BLACK HEART        8050       0.7460870 #> 2    💞     REVOLVING HEARTS         687       0.7423581 #> 3    🎉         PARTY POPPER        1125       0.7395556 #> 4    💃               DANCER        1344       0.7358631 #> 5    💙           BLUE HEART         912       0.7324561 #> 6    💖      SPARKLING HEART        1263       0.7133808 #> 7    💛         YELLOW HEART         602       0.7126246 #> 8    😘 FACE THROWING A KISS        3648       0.7017544  emoji_sentiment_lexicon %>%   filter(occurrences >= 500) %>%   slice_min(sentiment_score, n = 8) %>%   select(emoji, unicode_name, occurrences, sentiment_score) #>   emoji       unicode_name occurrences sentiment_score #> 1    😒      UNAMUSED FACE        1385     -0.37472924 #> 2    😩         WEARY FACE        1808     -0.36836283 #> 3    🔫             PISTOL         604     -0.19536424 #> 4    😡       POUTING FACE         756     -0.17328042 #> 5    😔       PENSIVE FACE        1205     -0.14605809 #> 6    😞  DISAPPOINTED FACE         532     -0.11842105 #> 7    😭 LOUDLY CRYING FACE        5526     -0.09337676 #> 8    😴      SLEEPING FACE         718     -0.08077994

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: tidyEmoji ships three datasets, documented help page: emoji_sentiment_lexicon — emoji sentiment scores Emoji Sentiment Ranking (see ?emoji_sentiment_lexicon). emoji_unicode_crosswalk — one row per emoji name, mapping names / shortcodes glyphs categories. category_unicode_crosswalk — one row per Unicode category, listing emoji. regenerated current Unicode emoji list scripts package’s data-raw/ directory.

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: Kralj Novak P, Smailović J, Sluban B, Mozetič (2015). Sentiment Emojis. PLoS ONE 10(12): e0144296. https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0144296. Emoji Sentiment Ranking distributed Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International (CC -SA 4.0) licence.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Youzhi Yu. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Yu Y (2026). tidyEmoji: Discover, Count Score Emoji Text. R package version 0.2.0, https://pursuitofdatascience.github.io/tidyEmoji/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {tidyEmoji: Discover, Count and Score Emoji in Text},   author = {Youzhi Yu},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.2.0},   url = {https://pursuitofdatascience.github.io/tidyEmoji/}, }

what: Discover, Count and Score Emoji in Text
title: Discover, Count and Score Emoji in Text
text: tidyEmoji helps discover, count, categorise sentiment-score emoji text column — social-media posts, product reviews, chat logs, survey responses, support tickets — hands result back tidy data frames drop straight tidyverse workflow. Unicode awkward work every code point emoji, makes emoji statistics fiddly. tidyEmoji takes care , including grapheme-aware detection skin-tone modifiers (👍🏽) multi-person sequences (👨‍👩‍👧‍👦) treated single emoji rather split apart.

title: Discover, Count and Score Emoji in Text
code: install.packages("tidyEmoji")  # development version # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("PursuitOfDataScience/tidyEmoji")

title: Discover, Count and Score Emoji in Text
code: library(tidyEmoji) library(dplyr)  reviews <- data.frame(text = c("Best purchase ever \U0001f600\U0001f60d",                                "It broke after a day \U0001f621",                                "Does the job.",                                "Wearing my mask \U0001f637\U0001f637",                                "Shipped fast \U0001f3c1\U0001f600"))

what: How much emoji is in the data?
title: Discover, Count and Score Emoji in Text
code: reviews %>% emoji_summary(text)        # entries with emoji vs. total #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   emoji_tweets total_tweets #>          <int>        <int> #> 1            4            5 reviews %>% emoji_filter(text)         # keep only the rows that have emoji #> # A tibble: 4 × 1 #>   text                    #>   <chr>                   #> 1 Best purchase ever 😀😍 #> 2 It broke after a day 😡 #> 3 Wearing my mask 😷😷    #> 4 Shipped fast 🏁😀

title: Discover, Count and Score Emoji in Text
code: reviews %>% emoji_frequency(text)      # every emoji, with name + category #> # A tibble: 5 × 5 #>   emoji name                         shortcode      group                 n #>   <chr> <chr>                        <chr>          <chr>             <int> #> 1 😀    grinning face                grinning       Smileys & Emotion     2 #> 2 😷    face with medical mask       mask           Smileys & Emotion     2 #> 3 🏁    chequered flag               checkered_flag Flags                 1 #> 4 😍    smiling face with heart-eyes heart_eyes     Smileys & Emotion     1 #> 5 😡    enraged face                 rage           Smileys & Emotion     1 reviews %>% top_n_emojis(text, n = 3)  # just the most frequent #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>   emoji_name     unicode emoji_category        n #>   <chr>          <chr>   <chr>             <int> #> 1 grinning       😀      Smileys & Emotion     2 #> 2 mask           😷      Smileys & Emotion     2 #> 3 checkered_flag 🏁      Flags                 1

title: Discover, Count and Score Emoji in Text
text: emoji_tokens() gives one tidy row per emoji occurrence, name, category sentiment — ready count, join plot.
code: reviews %>% emoji_tokens(text) #> # A tibble: 7 × 5 #>   text                    .emoji .emoji_name    .emoji_category .emoji_sentiment #>   <chr>                   <chr>  <chr>          <chr>                      <dbl> #> 1 Best purchase ever 😀😍 😀     grinning face  Smileys & Emot…            0.572 #> 2 Best purchase ever 😀😍 😍     smiling face … Smileys & Emot…            0.678 #> 3 It broke after a day 😡 😡     enraged face   Smileys & Emot…           -0.173 #> 4 Wearing my mask 😷😷    😷     face with med… Smileys & Emot…           -0.171 #> 5 Wearing my mask 😷😷    😷     face with med… Smileys & Emot…           -0.171 #> 6 Shipped fast 🏁😀       🏁     chequered flag Flags                      0.571 #> 7 Shipped fast 🏁😀       😀     grinning face  Smileys & Emot…            0.572

title: Discover, Count and Score Emoji in Text
text: emoji_sentiment() uses bundled Emoji Sentiment Ranking lexicon (Kralj Novak et al., 2015). See package vignette fuller tour.
code: reviews %>% emoji_categorize(text)     # which Unicode categories each row spans #> # A tibble: 4 × 2 #>   text                    .emoji_category         #>   <chr>                   <chr>                   #> 1 Best purchase ever 😀😍 Smileys & Emotion       #> 2 It broke after a day 😡 Smileys & Emotion       #> 3 Wearing my mask 😷😷    Smileys & Emotion       #> 4 Shipped fast 🏁😀       Flags|Smileys & Emotion reviews %>% emoji_sentiment(text)      # mean emoji sentiment per row (-1 to +1) #> # A tibble: 5 × 3 #>   text                    .emoji_n .emoji_sentiment #>   <chr>                      <int>            <dbl> #> 1 Best purchase ever 😀😍        2            0.625 #> 2 It broke after a day 😡        1           -0.173 #> 3 Does the job.                  0           NA     #> 4 Wearing my mask 😷😷           2           -0.171 #> 5 Shipped fast 🏁😀              2            0.572

what: Emoji category to unicode crosswalk — category_unicode_crosswalk
title: Emoji category to unicode crosswalk — category_unicode_crosswalk
text: table one row per Unicode category, listing every emoji glyph category single |-separated string.

title: Emoji category to unicode crosswalk — category_unicode_crosswalk
code: category_unicode_crosswalk

title: Emoji category to unicode crosswalk — category_unicode_crosswalk
text: data frame two columns: category Unicode category (10 categories). unicodes emoji glyphs category, separated |.

title: Emoji category to unicode crosswalk — category_unicode_crosswalk
text: Derived emojis table emoji package; rebuilt data-raw/crosswalks.R.

what: Categorise each row by the emoji categories it contains — emoji_categorize
title: Categorise each row by the emoji categories it contains — emoji_categorize
text: emoji_categorize() keeps rows data contain emoji adds .emoji_category column listing distinct Unicode categories present row (example "Smileys & Emotion"), separated | row spans one category.

title: Categorise each row by the emoji categories it contains — emoji_categorize
code: emoji_categorize(data, text)

title: Categorise each row by the emoji categories it contains — emoji_categorize
text: data data frame tibble containing text column. text text column scan, supplied unquoted.

title: Categorise each row by the emoji categories it contains — emoji_categorize
text: data, tibble, filtered rows containing emoji added .emoji_category column.

title: Categorise each row by the emoji categories it contains — emoji_categorize
code: df <- data.frame(text = c("smile \U0001f600",                           "flag \U0001f3c1\U0001f600",                           "nothing")) emoji_categorize(df, text) #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>   text      .emoji_category         #>   <chr>     <chr>                   #> 1 smile 😀  Smileys & Emotion       #> 2 flag 🏁😀 Flags|Smileys & Emotion

what: Add a list-column of the emoji found in each row — emoji_extract_nest
title: Add a list-column of the emoji found in each row — emoji_extract_nest
text: emoji_extract_nest() returns data unchanged except added list-column, .emoji_unicode, holding emoji found row. Detection grapheme-aware, skin-tone modifiers ZWJ sequences (example family emoji) kept intact single emoji.

title: Add a list-column of the emoji found in each row — emoji_extract_nest
code: emoji_extract_nest(data, text)

title: Add a list-column of the emoji found in each row — emoji_extract_nest
text: data data frame tibble containing text column. text text column scan, supplied unquoted.

title: Add a list-column of the emoji found in each row — emoji_extract_nest
text: data added list-column .emoji_unicode.

title: Add a list-column of the emoji found in each row — emoji_extract_nest
code: df <- data.frame(text = c("hi \U0001f600\U0001f603", "none")) emoji_extract_nest(df, text) #>      text .emoji_unicode #> 1 hi 😀😃         😀, 😃 #> 2    none

what: Emoji counts per row, in long (tidy) form — emoji_extract_unnest
title: Emoji counts per row, in long (tidy) form — emoji_extract_unnest
text: emoji_extract_unnest() returns one row per (row, emoji) pair count, dropping rows contain emoji. row_number refers position entry data.

title: Emoji counts per row, in long (tidy) form — emoji_extract_unnest
code: emoji_extract_unnest(data, text)

title: Emoji counts per row, in long (tidy) form — emoji_extract_unnest
text: data data frame tibble containing text column. text text column scan, supplied unquoted.

title: Emoji counts per row, in long (tidy) form — emoji_extract_unnest
text: tibble columns row_number, .emoji_unicode .emoji_count.

title: Emoji counts per row, in long (tidy) form — emoji_extract_unnest
code: df <- data.frame(text = c("hi \U0001f600\U0001f600", "none", "\U0001f44b")) emoji_extract_unnest(df, text) #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   row_number .emoji_unicode .emoji_count #>        <int> <chr>                 <int> #> 1          1 😀                        2 #> 2          3 👋                        1

what: Keep only the rows whose text contains emoji — emoji_filter
title: Keep only the rows whose text contains emoji — emoji_filter
text: emoji_filter() returns rows data whose text column contains least one emoji, preserving every original column. emoji_tweets() synonym retained backward compatibility.

title: Keep only the rows whose text contains emoji — emoji_filter
code: emoji_filter(data, text)  emoji_tweets(data, text)

title: Keep only the rows whose text contains emoji — emoji_filter
text: data data frame tibble containing text column. text text column scan, supplied unquoted.

title: Keep only the rows whose text contains emoji — emoji_filter
text: tibble containing rows least one emoji.

title: Keep only the rows whose text contains emoji — emoji_filter
code: df <- data.frame(text = c("hi \U0001f600", "no emoji", "bye \U0001f44b")) emoji_filter(df, text) #> # A tibble: 2 × 1 #>   text   #>   <chr>  #> 1 hi 😀  #> 2 bye 👋

what: Frequency of every emoji in a text column — emoji_frequency
title: Frequency of every emoji in a text column — emoji_frequency
text: emoji_frequency() counts often emoji appears across whole text column (entry containing emoji twice contributes 2) returns tibble sorted descending count, emoji's name, shortcode category.

title: Frequency of every emoji in a text column — emoji_frequency
code: emoji_frequency(data, text)

title: Frequency of every emoji in a text column — emoji_frequency
text: data data frame tibble containing text column. text text column scan, supplied unquoted.

title: Frequency of every emoji in a text column — emoji_frequency
text: tibble columns emoji, name, shortcode, group n.

title: Frequency of every emoji in a text column — emoji_frequency
code: df <- data.frame(text = c("\U0001f600\U0001f600", "\U0001f621")) emoji_frequency(df, text) #> # A tibble: 2 × 5 #>   emoji name          shortcode group                 n #>   <chr> <chr>         <chr>     <chr>             <int> #> 1 😀    grinning face grinning  Smileys & Emotion     2 #> 2 😡    enraged face  rage      Smileys & Emotion     1

what: Score the sentiment of the emoji in each row — emoji_sentiment
title: Score the sentiment of the emoji in each row — emoji_sentiment
text: emoji_sentiment() adds mean emoji sentiment row, based Emoji Sentiment Ranking lexicon (see emoji_sentiment_lexicon). Scores range -1 (negative) 0 (neutral) +1 (positive). Rows contain emoji, whose emoji absent lexicon, receive NA.

title: Score the sentiment of the emoji in each row — emoji_sentiment
code: emoji_sentiment(data, text)

title: Score the sentiment of the emoji in each row — emoji_sentiment
text: data data frame tibble containing text column. text text column scan, supplied unquoted.

title: Score the sentiment of the emoji in each row — emoji_sentiment
text: data, tibble, added columns .emoji_n (number emoji row) .emoji_sentiment (mean sentiment emoji appear lexicon).

title: Score the sentiment of the emoji in each row — emoji_sentiment
text: Kralj Novak P, Smailovic J, Sluban B, Mozetic (2015) Sentiment Emojis. PLoS ONE 10(12): e0144296. doi:10.1371/journal.pone.0144296

title: Score the sentiment of the emoji in each row — emoji_sentiment
code: df <- data.frame(text = c("love it \U0001f60d", "awful \U0001f621", "meh")) emoji_sentiment(df, text) #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>   text       .emoji_n .emoji_sentiment #>   <chr>         <int>            <dbl> #> 1 love it 😍        1            0.678 #> 2 awful 😡          1           -0.173 #> 3 meh               0           NA

title: Emoji Sentiment Ranking lexicon — emoji_sentiment_lexicon
text: Sentiment scores emoji, Emoji Sentiment Ranking 1.0, computed ~70,000 tweets 13 European languages annotated sentiment. sentiment_score (positive - negative) / occurrences, ranging -1 (negative) +1 (positive); sentiment_label derived sign.

title: Emoji Sentiment Ranking lexicon — emoji_sentiment_lexicon
code: emoji_sentiment_lexicon

title: Emoji Sentiment Ranking lexicon — emoji_sentiment_lexicon
text: data frame one row per emoji columns: emoji emoji glyph. occurrences Number times emoji observed. position Mean position emoji within text (0-1). negative, neutral, positive Annotation counts class. sentiment_score Sentiment score -1 1. sentiment_label "negative", "neutral" "positive". unicode_name official Unicode character name. unicode_block Unicode block.

title: Emoji Sentiment Ranking lexicon — emoji_sentiment_lexicon
text: Kralj Novak P, Smailovic J, Sluban B, Mozetic (2015) Sentiment Emojis. PLoS ONE 10(12): e0144296. doi:10.1371/journal.pone.0144296 . Data https://hdl.handle.net/11356/1048, released Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International (CC -SA 4.0) licence. Processed data-raw/emoji_sentiment_lexicon.R.

what: Summarise emoji presence in a text column — emoji_summary
title: Summarise emoji presence in a text column — emoji_summary
text: emoji_summary() reports many entries text column contain least one emoji, alongside total number entries. entry counted regardless many emoji holds.

title: Summarise emoji presence in a text column — emoji_summary
code: emoji_summary(data, text)

title: Summarise emoji presence in a text column — emoji_summary
text: data data frame tibble containing text column. text text column scan, supplied unquoted.

title: Summarise emoji presence in a text column — emoji_summary
text: one-row tibble columns emoji_tweets (entries containing least one emoji) total_tweets (entries).

title: Summarise emoji presence in a text column — emoji_summary
code: df <- data.frame(text = c("I love R \U0001f600",                           "no emoji here",                           "flags \U0001f3c1\U0001f600")) emoji_summary(df, text) #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   emoji_tweets total_tweets #>          <int>        <int> #> 1            2            3

what: Tidy emoji tokens, one row per occurrence with metadata — emoji_tokens
title: Tidy emoji tokens, one row per occurrence with metadata — emoji_tokens
text: emoji_tokens() expands data one row per emoji occurrence (reading order), keeping original columns adding glyph together name, category sentiment score. mirrors one-token-per-row shape familiar tidy text mining convenient counting, joining plotting.

title: Tidy emoji tokens, one row per occurrence with metadata — emoji_tokens
code: emoji_tokens(data, text)

title: Tidy emoji tokens, one row per occurrence with metadata — emoji_tokens
text: data data frame tibble containing text column. text text column scan, supplied unquoted.

title: Tidy emoji tokens, one row per occurrence with metadata — emoji_tokens
text: tibble original columns plus .emoji, .emoji_name, .emoji_category .emoji_sentiment. Rows without emoji dropped.

title: Tidy emoji tokens, one row per occurrence with metadata — emoji_tokens
code: df <- data.frame(id = 1:2, text = c("great \U0001f600", "bad \U0001f621")) emoji_tokens(df, text) #> # A tibble: 2 × 6 #>      id text     .emoji .emoji_name   .emoji_category   .emoji_sentiment #>   <int> <chr>    <chr>  <chr>         <chr>                        <dbl> #> 1     1 great 😀 😀     grinning face Smileys & Emotion            0.572 #> 2     2 bad 😡   😡     enraged face  Smileys & Emotion           -0.173

what: Emoji name, unicode and category crosswalk — emoji_unicode_crosswalk
title: Emoji name, unicode and category crosswalk — emoji_unicode_crosswalk
text: table one row per emoji name: emoji glyph appears every GitHub-style name known , single unicode can occur several rows (example grinning face "grinning" "grinning_face").

title: Emoji name, unicode and category crosswalk — emoji_unicode_crosswalk
code: emoji_unicode_crosswalk

title: Emoji name, unicode and category crosswalk — emoji_unicode_crosswalk
text: data frame three columns: emoji_name emoji name / shortcode (e.g. "grinning"). unicode emoji glyph. emoji_category Unicode category emoji belongs .

title: Emoji name, unicode and category crosswalk — emoji_unicode_crosswalk
text: Derived emojis table emoji package; rebuilt data-raw/crosswalks.R.

what: tidyEmoji: Discover, Count and Score Emoji in Text — tidyEmoji-package
title: tidyEmoji: Discover, Count and Score Emoji in Text — tidyEmoji-package
text: tidy toolkit working emoji text column, social-media posts, product reviews, chat logs survey responses. Unicode awkward handle every code point emoji, makes emoji statistics fiddly obtain. 'tidyEmoji' extracts, counts, categorises sentiment-scores emoji grapheme-aware detection (skin-tone multi-person sequences stay intact), returning tidy data frames slot straight 'tidyverse' workflow. bundled emoji sentiment lexicon Emoji Sentiment Ranking Kralj Novak et al. (2015) doi:10.1371/journal.pone.0144296 , released CC -SA 4.0.

title: tidyEmoji: Discover, Count and Score Emoji in Text — tidyEmoji-package
text: Maintainer: Youzhi Yu yuyouzhi666@icloud.com

what: The most frequent emoji in a text column — top_n_emojis
title: The most frequent emoji in a text column — top_n_emojis
text: top_n_emojis() returns n frequent emoji. default emoji (unicode) appears single row; set duplicated = TRUE list every name emoji known , glyphs share several names occupy several rows.

title: The most frequent emoji in a text column — top_n_emojis
code: top_n_emojis(   data,   text,   n = 20,   duplicated = FALSE,   duplicated_unicode = lifecycle::deprecated() )

title: The most frequent emoji in a text column — top_n_emojis
text: data data frame tibble containing text column. text text column scan, supplied unquoted. n Number emoji return. Default 20. duplicated TRUE, emoji several names occupy several rows. Default FALSE. duplicated_unicode Use duplicated instead.

title: The most frequent emoji in a text column — top_n_emojis
text: tibble columns emoji_name, unicode, emoji_category n.

title: The most frequent emoji in a text column — top_n_emojis
code: df <- data.frame(text = c("\U0001f600\U0001f600\U0001f3c1", "\U0001f621")) top_n_emojis(df, text, n = 2) #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   emoji_name     unicode emoji_category        n #>   <chr>          <chr>   <chr>             <int> #> 1 grinning       😀      Smileys & Emotion     2 #> 2 checkered_flag 🏁      Flags                 1

title: tidyEmoji 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-17 tidyEmoji now positioned general toolkit emoji text column (social-media posts, reviews, chat logs, survey responses, …), just tweets.

title: tidyEmoji 0.2.0
text: emoji_sentiment() scores emoji row using bundled emoji_sentiment_lexicon (Emoji Sentiment Ranking Kralj Novak et al., 2015), returning mean sentiment [-1, 1]. emoji_frequency() returns count every emoji text column, name, shortcode category. top_n_emojis() now thin wrapper . emoji_tokens() expands data one row per emoji occurrence name, category sentiment score — tidy, “one-token-per-row” shape. emoji_filter() clearer, text-agnostic name emoji_tweets() (kept synonym). New bundled dataset emoji_sentiment_lexicon.

title: tidyEmoji 0.2.0
text: Grapheme-aware detection. Extraction now keeps skin-tone modifiers zero-width-joiner sequences intact. Previously family emoji (👨‍👩‍👧‍👦) miscounted four separate people skin-tone thumbs-split two “emoji”; now counted one. Much faster. Detection counting longer build multi-thousand-way regular expression every call scan text per known emoji; top_n_emojis() particular dramatically faster large inputs. top_n_emojis() longer emits many--many join warning, reports emoji’s canonical shortcode (e.g. mask) default. verbs now return tibbles consistently (emoji_tweets() previously returned plain data frame), emoji_extract_unnest() longer prints grouping message. Bundled emoji data refreshed current Unicode emoji list (via data-raw/).

title: tidyEmoji 0.2.0
text: Arguments renamed tweet_tbl -> data tweet_text -> text. Code passed positionally (e.g. df %>% emoji_summary(text_col)) unaffected; update calls named old arguments. top_n_emojis(duplicated_unicode = "yes"/"") deprecated favour logical duplicated = TRUE/FALSE. old argument still works warning.

title: tidyEmoji 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2023-08-19 Changed package metadata

title: tidyEmoji 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-02-18 Initial release CRAN.
